sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current state:
- Node 9 is now M (magenta) at (36,17) - was S at (37,18). It changed from S to M and expanded from 1 pixel to 4 pixels.
The remaining S dots are at: (37,41), (40,37), (54,49), (55,14), (56,47), (57,16)
The top bar has: S(1,1), M(1,5), p(1,9), Y(1,13), Y(4,31)
So the pattern seems to be: click S dots and they change to M. But there are 7 S dots and only 4 colored squares at the top.
Wait, the top bar colors are: S, M, p, Y. Maybe each is a 2x2 square (4 pixels). The Y at (4,31) is 16 pixels so it's 4x4.
Let me think about this differently. In level 1, I clicked gray pixels along a diagonal path. The white cross was the starting point. Here, maybe the blue circle is the "shoots" at the S dots and they turn pink/magenta.
Actually, looking at the image, the dot at (37,18) is now pink. The top bar has pink square is at (1,5). Maybe I need to click all S dots need to be clicked to turn them all to M (magenta). Let me try clicking all S dots to M. Let me try clicking the remaining S dots.

# Click all remaining S dots to turn them to M
remaining_S = [(37,41), (40,37), (54,49), (55,14), (56,47), (57,16)]
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in remaining_S]
action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
